title: luzula alpinopilosa subsp. velenovskyi
intro: Luzula alpinopilosa subsp. velenovskyi, also known as Velenovsky's Woodrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of the Czech Republic and Slovakia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and prevent infection. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote wound healing, reduce swelling, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs.
